mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal collapsing consumer demand that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 52 percent across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month expansions in industrial production indicate supply-side recovery that resolves inventory bottlenecks, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering margin calls and equity deleveraging that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day S&P 500 returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 indicates severe supply chain contraction that forces production cuts and earnings warnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO drops below 90.0 during late-cycle expansions, with a p-value exceeding 0.10 on the directional test.
